description_long: This object is an old, weathered candle mounted on a sturdy, circular metal candlestick holder. The candle itself is short and stubby, with a visible wick protruding from the top and some signs of melted wax around its base. The holder features a wide, flat drip plate beneath the candle, supported by a thick, vertical stem that connects to a broad, round base. The metal surfaces are tarnished and rusted, giving the object a rustic, antique appearance. The base is slightly raised and has a lip around the edge, with some areas showing chipped paint or corrosion. This type of candle holder would be suitable for outdoor or urban settings, such as on a park table, street-side memorial, or in a historical neighborhood.
description: A weathered, stubby candle sits atop a rusted metal candlestick holder with a wide, round base and drip plate. The object has a rustic, antique look with visible tarnish and corrosion.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the candle and the wide, circular base with visible rust and a raised lip.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the side of the candle and the depth of the drip plate, with more of the base's inner surface visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the straight alignment of the stem and the circular base, with the candle centered above.
description_view_3: Angled rear view emphasizing the candle's stubby shape and the uneven, corroded edge of the base.
